Test Case Description: Verify that the user can login successfully with valid Super Admin credentials.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter valid Super Admin username and password.
3. Click 'Login'.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the Dashboard and a success message is displayed.

Test Case Description: Verify that the 'Ticket Configuration' menu is visible in the sidebar.
Steps:
1. Look at the left sidebar navigation menu.
2. Locate the 'Ticket Configuration' item.
Expected Result: 'Ticket Configuration' is visible under the management/admin section.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Ticket Configuration' navigates to the project selection page.
Steps: 1. Click on 'Ticket Configuration' in the sidebar.
Expected Result: The system navigates to /ticket-config and displays the project list.

Test Case Description: Verify that the search bar correctly filters projects to show 'Auto12'.
Steps:
1. Locate the search bar.
2. Type 'Auto12'.
Expected Result: Only the 'Auto12' project card is displayed in the list.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking the 'Auto12' project card opens the configuration settings.
Steps: 1. Click on the 'Auto12' project card.
Expected Result: The system navigates to the detailed settings page for project 'Auto12'.

Test Case Description: Verify that the 'Ticket Numbering' tab is active by default.
Steps: 1. Observe the landing view of the settings page.
Expected Result: The 'Ticket Numbering' tab is highlighted and its fields are visible.

Test Case Description: Verify that entering a Prefix (e.g., 'A12') updates the preview format.
Steps:
1. Enter 'A12' in the Prefix textbox.
2. Observe the preview panel.
Expected Result: The preview reflects the new format: A12-2026-0001.

Test Case Description: Verify that entering a Starting Number (e.g., '100') updates the preview sequence.
Steps: 1. Enter '100' in the Starting Number field.
Expected Result: The preview updates to show the starting number (e.g., A12-2026-0100).

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting a Separator (e.g., '/') updates the preview correctly.
Steps: 1. Select '/' from the Separator dropdown.
Expected Result: The preview format changes to use the selected separator (e.g., A12/2026/0100).

Test Case Description: Verify that toggling 'Include Year' adds the current year to the preview.
Steps: 1. Toggle 'Include Year' to ON.
Expected Result: The current year (2026) is visible in the preview format.

Test Case Description: Verify that toggling 'Include Month' adds the current month to the preview.
Steps: 1. Toggle 'Include Month' to ON.
Expected Result: The current month is visible in the preview format.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting 'Reset Frequency' (Yearly/Monthly) is saved.
Steps: 1. Select 'Monthly' from the Reset Frequency dropdown.
Expected Result: The selection is accepted and displayed in the dropdown.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Save' persists the numbering settings.
Steps: 1. Click 'Save All Changes'.
Expected Result: A success toast 'Saved!' is displayed.

Test Case Description: Verify that after saving and refreshing, the numbering changes remain updated.
Steps: 1. Refresh the browser page.
Expected Result: Prefix, Starting Number, and Format remain exactly as saved previously.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking the 'Ticket Statuses' tab displays the list of statuses.
Steps: 1. Click on the 'Ticket Statuses' tab.
Expected Result: A table of all current statuses for 'Auto12' is displayed.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking '+ Add Status' opens the status creation modal.
Steps: 1. Click the '+ Add Status' button.
Expected Result: The 'Add Status' modal opens with empty fields.

Test Case Description: Verify that entering a Status Name auto-generates the Status Code.
Steps: 1. Type 'Pending Verification' in the Name field.
Expected Result: The Code field is automatically filled with 'PENDING_VERIFICATION'.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting a color from the palette updates the status icon preview.
Steps: 1. Select a custom color (e.g., Purple) from the color picker.
Expected Result: The status icon in the list/preview updates to the selected color.

Test Case Description: Verify that toggling 'Set as Default Status' marks it as the entry status.
Steps: 1. Toggle 'Set as Default Status' to ON.
Expected Result: The status is marked as default (only one status can be default).

Test Case Description: Verify that toggling 'This Status Closes Tickets' marks it as a terminal status.
Steps: 1. Toggle 'This Status Closes Tickets' to ON.
Expected Result: The status is marked as a closing status.

Test Case Description: Verify that saving a new status adds it to the table and summary.
Steps: 1. Click 'Save' in the modal.
Expected Result: The status 'Pending Verification' appears in the table and the summary panel.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Edit' allows modification of an existing status.
Steps:
1. Click 'Edit' on an existing status.
2. Change the color.
3. Click 'Save'.
Expected Result: The status color is updated successfully in the list.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Delete' removes the status from the project.
Steps:
1. Click 'Delete' on a status.
2. Confirm deletion.
Expected Result: The status is removed from the table and summary panel.

Test Case Description: Verify that [NEG] saving a status without a name displays a validation error.
Steps:
1. Open Add Status modal.
2. Leave Name empty.
3. Click 'Save'.
Expected Result: System displays 'Name is required' error message.

Test Case Description: Verify that all status changes persist after a page refresh.
Steps: 1. Refresh the page.
Expected Result: All added/edited statuses are correctly displayed.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking the 'Ticket Categories' tab displays all categories.
Steps: 1. Click on the 'Ticket Categories' tab.
Expected Result: List of categories for 'Auto12' is visible.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking '+ Add Category' opens the category modal.
Steps: 1. Click the '+ Add Category' button.
Expected Result: The 'Add Category' modal appears.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting a 'Default Priority' for a category is saved.
Steps: 1. Select 'High' from the Default Priority dropdown.
Expected Result: The priority selection is visible and saved.

Test Case Description: Verify that adding a description and color to a category works correctly.
Steps:
1. Enter a description.
2. Choose a color.
Expected Result: Description and color are assigned to the category correctly.

Test Case Description: Verify that toggling a category to 'Inactive' hides it from end-users.
Steps: 1. Toggle 'Active' switch to OFF.
Expected Result: The category appears grayed out in the list and is marked as Inactive.

Test Case Description: Verify that saving a new category adds it to the configuration summary.
Steps: 1. Click 'Save' in the modal.
Expected Result: The new category appears in the 'Configured Categories' list in the right panel.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Edit' allows updating category details.
Steps:
1. Click 'Edit' on a category.
2. Change priority to 'Medium'.
3. Save.
Expected Result: The category priority is updated successfully.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Delete' removes the category after confirmation.
Steps:
1. Click 'Delete' on a category.
2. Confirm.
Expected Result: The category is deleted from the list.

Test Case Description: Verify that [NEG] adding a category with a duplicate name displays an error.
Steps: 1. Attempt to add a category with an existing name.
Expected Result: System displays an error message regarding duplicate category.

Test Case Description: Verify that category changes are persisted after re-entering the project.
Steps:
1. Navigate back to Project list.
2. Re-enter 'Auto12' config.
Expected Result: All category changes are visible as saved.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking the 'Form Fields' tab shows the submission form config.
Steps: 1. Click on the 'Form Fields' tab.
Expected Result: Current custom fields for 'Auto12' are displayed.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking '+ Add Field' adds a new field row to the list.
Steps: 1. Click the '+ Add Field' button.
Expected Result: A new empty field row is added to the configuration list.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting 'Text' field type allows entering name and placeholder.
Steps:
1. Choose 'Text' type.
2. Enter Name 'Employee ID' and Placeholder 'Enter ID'.
Expected Result: The field settings are accepted.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting 'Dropdown' field type enables the 'Options' input area.
Steps: 1. Select 'Dropdown' as field type.
Expected Result: An 'Options' text area appears below the field type selector.

Test Case Description: Verify that entering comma-separated options for a dropdown is saved correctly.
Steps: 1. Enter 'HR, Finance, Tech' in options area.
Expected Result: The options are parsed and stored correctly.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting 'Date' field type is saved.
Steps: 1. Select 'Date' field type.
Expected Result: Field is configured as a date picker.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting 'File Upload' type allows setting size limits.
Steps:
1. Select 'File Upload' type.
2. Set limit to '10MB'.
Expected Result: File upload field is configured with 10MB limit.

Test Case Description: Verify that toggling 'Required' marks the field as mandatory in the UI.
Steps: 1. Toggle 'Required' checkbox to ON.
Expected Result: The field is marked as mandatory (*).

Test Case Description: Verify that reordering fields using the drag handle updates the display order.
Steps: 1. Click and drag a field handle to a new position.
Expected Result: The field row moves and maintains its new position in the list.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Delete' on a field row removes it from the form.
Steps: 1. Click the 'Delete' icon on a field row.
Expected Result: The field is immediately removed from the list.

Test Case Description: Verify that [NEG] saving a field without a name displays an error.
Steps:
1. Add a field.
2. Leave Name empty.
3. Click Save.
Expected Result: System displays 'Field name is required' error.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Save' persists the form fields to the project.
Steps: 1. Click 'Save All Changes'.
Expected Result: Success message is displayed.

Test Case Description: Verify that after saving, the form fields appear in the correct order after refresh.
Steps: 1. Refresh the page.
Expected Result: Form fields are displayed in the exact order and configuration saved.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking the 'Table Columns' tab shows all available columns.
Steps: 1. Click on the 'Table Columns' tab.
Expected Result: A list of system and custom columns is displayed with checkboxes.

Test Case Description: Verify that checking a column (e.g., 'Priority') makes it visible in the list.
Steps: 1. Check the 'Priority' checkbox.
Expected Result: The column is marked for visibility.

Test Case Description: Verify that unchecking a column removes it from the default view.
Steps: 1. Uncheck the 'Tags' checkbox.
Expected Result: The column is marked to be hidden.

Test Case Description: Verify that selecting 'Default Sort' (e.g., 'Ticket ID') is saved.
Steps: 1. Select 'Ticket ID' from Default Sort dropdown.
Expected Result: The sorting preference is accepted.

Test Case Description: Verify that changing 'Sort Direction' (Asc/Desc) is saved.
Steps: 1. Select 'Descending' from the sort order.
Expected Result: The sort direction is saved.

Test Case Description: Verify that system-required columns are locked and cannot be hidden.
Steps: 1. Attempt to uncheck 'Ticket ID'.
Expected Result: The checkbox for 'Ticket ID' is disabled/read-only.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Save' persists the table column configuration.
Steps: 1. Click 'Save All Changes'.
Expected Result: Success message is displayed.

Test Case Description: Verify that column settings remain unchanged after page navigation.
Steps: 1. Navigate away and return.
Expected Result: Column visibility and sorting settings remain as configured.

Test Case Description: Verify that the 'Save All Changes' button is enabled only when changes exist.
Steps:
1. Observe button state without changes.
2. Make a change.
3. Observe again.
Expected Result: Button is disabled initially; becomes enabled (purple) after any change.

Test Case Description: Verify that clicking 'Save All Changes' displays a global success notification.
Steps: 1. Click the main 'Save All Changes' button.
Expected Result: A toast/alert confirming all settings are updated appears.

Test Case Description: Verify that all changes across all 5 tabs are reflected after a full logout/login.
Steps:
1. Log out.
2. Log in.
3. Check all tabs in 'Auto12'.
Expected Result: All configurations across Numbering, Statuses, Categories, Fields, and Columns are persisted.

Test Case Description: Verify that changes made to project 'Auto12' do not affect other projects.
Steps: 1. Open configuration for a different project (e.g., 'Demo').
Expected Result: The configuration settings for 'Demo' remain in their original state.
